name: OpenClaw Feishu Multi-Agent
tagline: Make Feishu multi-agent workflows actually work.
description:
OpenClaw + Feishu integrations often fail at the handoff layer: a bot can visibly mention another bot in the group, but that does not actually wake the other agent. This skill codifies the correct pattern: use Feishu `<at>` for human-visible coordination and `sessions_send` for real agent-to-agent delivery.

The bundle includes production-oriented docs, role templates, and Python utilities for rendering configs from a roles file, auditing existing OpenClaw setups, repairing broken Feishu group session metadata, and applying changes with a dry-run-first workflow. The architecture is role-driven, so users can define any coordinator and specialist team instead of being locked into fixed personas.
